page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the n4d server family. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance does not include any local storage or integrated GPUs, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

This server configuration is designed for compute-intensive workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of local storage or GPU acceleration. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a resource-dense profile optimized for processing power rather than memory capacity. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the lack of bundled local storage and GPUs minimizes baseline hardware costs, making it a targeted option for pure compute tasks where external storage is attached separately.

Given the 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, the n4d-highcpu-32 is suited for workloads such as batch processing, compute-heavy web serving, build systems, and distributed analytics that do not require high memory footprints or local disk storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized server instance from the n4d family, built on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for compute-heavy tasks, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance. Qualitatively, the hardware profile offers a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring high CPU density without the added expense of integrated storage or graphics hardware. It is suited for applications such as batch processing, compute-intensive web serving, and compilation tasks.
